suboptimal_algorithms: O(N^2) Nested Loop Scan
O(N^3) Brute Force Subarrays
Array.edge_cases: Array size 1, Size 0, All 0s, All Identical, All Negative, Max Integer values
Array.logic_traps: Out of bounds access on n-1, off-by-one in loops

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N^2) string concatenation
O(N^2) naive .find()
String.edge_cases: Empty string, Length 1, No matches present, Entire string is one character
String.logic_traps: Handling spaces, special characters, case sensitivity

suboptimal_algorithms: Storing full elements instead of indices when not needed
Unbounded stack growth reaching O(N) memory
Stack.large_adversarial: Strictly increasing or decreasing sequences that delay resolving items
Stack.logic_traps: Failing to check if stack is empty before popping

suboptimal_algorithms: Using array O(N) shift instead of proper O(1) Queue struct
Queue.edge_cases: Empty queue polling, rapid enqueue/dequeue flushes
Queue.large_adversarial: Massive alternating queues tracking window minimums but storing useless data
Queue.logic_traps: Off by one on current size bounds

suboptimal_algorithms: Re-sorting entire array on each insert O(N log N) instead of O(log N) heap insert
Heap.large_adversarial: Inserting strictly increasing values into min-heap to force worst-case sift-up

suboptimal_algorithms: Greedy choice selection causing WA
Missing DP dimension (state reduction assumption)
O(2^N) pure recursion without memoization
Dynamic Programming.edge_cases: N=0, N=1, Choices with 0 cost, Extreme high cost blocking paths
Dynamic Programming.large_adversarial: Massive linear paths where Greedy takes local optimal but fails global, deep recursion trees
Dynamic Programming.logic_traps: Integer overflow on DP table, base case not initialized correctly

suboptimal_algorithms: DFS infinite loop no visited array
O(V^3) shortest path instead of O(E log V)
BFS memory limit exceeded
Graph.edge_cases: Disconnected graph, Single node, Graph with cycles, Self-loops, Multiple edges
Graph.large_adversarial: Dense Star Graph (bottleneck), Degenerate Line 1->2->3...V (Stack Overflow for DFS), Bipartite worst-case
Graph.logic_traps: Assuming connected components, assuming 1-based indexing, forgetting directed vs undirected

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N) prime checking instead of O(sqrt(N))
Iterative GCD instead of Euclidean
Math.edge_cases: N=0, N=1, Negative numbers, Prime numbers, MAX_INT
Math.large_adversarial: Massive primes, Products of two large primes
Math.logic_traps: Modulo arithmetic errors, Integer overflow, floating point precision loss

suboptimal_algorithms: Suboptimal greedy metric (e.g., sort by weight instead of ratio)
Greedy.edge_cases: All items same value, Only one choice possible
Greedy.large_adversarial: Inputs mathematically designed to make the local greedy choice produce the worst global outcome

suboptimal_algorithms: Generating all permutations instead of pruning invalid branches early
Backtracking.edge_cases: No solution possible, All variables constrain to one path immediately
Backtracking.large_adversarial: Massive search spaces where the valid solution is at the absolute end of the tree
Backtracking.logic_traps: Not reverting state (un-doing the move) after exploring a branch

suboptimal_algorithms: Bubble Sort O(N^2)
Selection Sort O(N^2)
Sorting.edge_cases: Already sorted, Reverse sorted, All identical
Sorting.large_adversarial: 100% Identical elements (kills quicksort pivots), Anti-quicksort organ pipe distributions
Sorting.logic_traps: Unstable sorting when stability is required

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N) Linear scan instead of O(log N) Binary Search
Searching.edge_cases: Element not in array, Search range 0, Midpoint calculations overflowing
Searching.large_adversarial: Value to find is exactly outside the initial search space parameters
Searching.logic_traps: Infinite loops with mid = (L+R)/2 on two elements, missing target at indices L or R

suboptimal_algorithms: Re-hashing repeatedly with improper load factor management
O(N^2) linear searching to find duplicates
Hash Table.edge_cases: Zero elements, 10^5 elements all hashing to same bucket (Hash Collisions)
Hash Table.large_adversarial: Data structures crafted maliciously with high collision rates in standard language hash maps
Hash Table.logic_traps: Iterating over hash map assumes ordered keys, referencing undefined properties

suboptimal_algorithms: High branching factor without pruning, O(Branch^Depth)
Recursion.large_adversarial: Degenerated extreme depths forcing Stack Overflow limits (10^4 call stack)
Recursion.logic_traps: Missing base cases, not properly returning recursive results

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N) character string binary conversions instead of bitwise operations
Bit Manipulation.edge_cases: Zero, power of 2 flags, negative numbers representing two's complement
Bit Manipulation.large_adversarial: Integers exactly at the 32-bit or 64-bit bounds to test signed/unsigned shifts
Bit Manipulation.logic_traps: Using logical shift vs arithmetic shift incorrectly, assuming 32-bit limits instead of 64-bit

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N^2) restarting window from scratch
O(N^2) nested loops instead of moving pointers
Two Pointers.edge_cases: Window size larger than array, Window size 0, K=0
Two Pointers.large_adversarial: Array forcing pointer resets constantly, lots of duplicate elements requiring careful pointer skipping
Two Pointers.logic_traps: Moving the wrong pointer on equals conditions, out of bounds loops

suboptimal_algorithms: Re-summing entire window on each step O(N^2) instead of O(1) state math
Sliding Window.large_adversarial: Massive zero or negative fluctuations forcing continuous shrink/expand logic errors
Sliding Window.logic_traps: Updating max/min before shrinking the window causing invalid states

suboptimal_algorithms: Trivial recursion failing to achieve O(N log N) overhead speedups
Divide and Conquer.edge_cases: Base case of size less than 2
Divide and Conquer.large_adversarial: Already solved states that force worst-case splits if pivots are handled poorly
Divide and Conquer.logic_traps: Combining subproblems in O(N^2) rather than O(N)

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N) find operations without path compression
Union without rank/size heuristic
Union Find.edge_cases: No components merged, all nodes are their own parent
Union Find.large_adversarial: Long chained unions mapping 1-2, 2-3, 3-4 to build an O(N) depth tree without path compression
Union Find.logic_traps: Forgetting to initialize parent arrays properly, returning node instead of parent[node]

suboptimal_algorithms: Unnecessary intermediate string parsing for cells, O(R^2 * C^2) submatrix scans
Matrix.large_adversarial: Massive grids filled with a single value, checkerboard patterns causing high DFS branching
Matrix.logic_traps: Out of bounds on [r+dr][c+dc], assuming square dimensions instead of R!=C

suboptimal_algorithms: Simulating every millisecond instead of jumping to the next event time
Simulation.large_adversarial: Conditions requiring billions of steps if simulated deterministically (forces modulo or cycle detection approaches)
Simulation.logic_traps: Not cleanly breaking while-loops on steady state convergence

suboptimal_algorithms: Minimax without memoization, full search space mapping
Game Theory.edge_cases: Winning condition already met on turn 1
Game Theory.large_adversarial: Extremely large Nim setups requiring XOR sums rather than DP tree states
Game Theory.logic_traps: Calculating for the wrong player turn, confusing parity logic

suboptimal_algorithms: O(V^3) dependency walks, failing to use Kahn's or complete DFS
Topological Sort.edge_cases: Forest of independent nodes, exact cycle present
Topological Sort.large_adversarial: Massive fully connected DAGs rendering incorrect edge counts fatal
Topological Sort.logic_traps: Not detecting cycles correctly and outputting invalid sorts

suboptimal_algorithms: Nested O(N^2) loops to find 'next greater element' instead of O(N) monotonic stack
Monotonic Stack.edge_cases: All elements ascending, All elements descending, Identical elements
Monotonic Stack.large_adversarial: Mountain/valley inputs making elements sit in the stack indefinitely until the last possible array index
Monotonic Stack.logic_traps: Popping conditions incorrect ( > vs >=), failing to process remaining stack items after array ends

suboptimal_algorithms: (Alias for Two Pointers) O(N^2) nested loops
Two Pointer.large_adversarial: Long repetitive sequences blocking pointer closures
Two Pointer.logic_traps: Moving left/right incorrectly on equal values

suboptimal_algorithms: O(N) summation repeatedly inside a Q query loop instead of O(1) subset differences
Prefix Sum.large_adversarial: 10^5 rapid queries over maximum span integer bounds to induce absolute high IO and math stress
Prefix Sum.logic_traps: Off-by-one errors like prefix[R+1] - prefix[L], assuming 0-based index matches 1-based prefix arrays
